title: Elaphoglossum erinaceum
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum erinaceum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Elaphoglossum, which comprises over 1000 species of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns or shield ferns. Elaphoglossum erinaceum is native to Central and South America, where it grows in shaded, humid environments. Its classification is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics, which are typical of the Polypodiales order.

intro: Elaphoglossum erinaceum is a species of fern, specifically a type of epiphytic fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. Research has shown that Elaphoglossum erinaceum possesses various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory disorders.
